body:
The flux computation is failing because of type conversion which leads to `u★` having a different type than `u` in `InterfaceStates`. In particular, the error is 
```julia
ERROR: LoadError: MethodError: no method matching InterfaceState(::Float64, ::Float64, ::Float64, ::Float32, ::Float32, ::Float32, ::Float32, ::Float64, ::Bool)

Closest candidates are:
  InterfaceState(::Any, ::Any, ::Any, ::Any, ::Any, ::Any, ::Any, ::Any)
   @ ClimaOcean ~/development/ClimaOcean.jl/src/OceanSeaIceModels/InterfaceComputations/interface_states.jl:374
  InterfaceState(::FT1, ::FT1, ::FT1, ::FT2, ::FT2, ::FT2, ::FT2, ::FT2, ::Bool) where {FT1, FT2}
   @ ClimaOcean ~/development/ClimaOcean.jl/src/OceanSeaIceModels/InterfaceComputations/interface_states.jl:363
```
because in this case, the atmosphere, the ocean, and the interface have different float types. 
I think we are missing the possibility of changing float types in some constructors, so I'll take a look at that later.
In the meantime, this PR ensures the code does not error when the types are different for the different components.
title: Convert type in `InterfaceState`
